skill.name: refactor
skill.description: Surgical code refactoring to improve maintainability without changing behavior. Covers extracting functions, renaming variables, breaking down god functions, improving type safety, eliminating code smells, and applying design patterns. Less drastic than repo-rebuilder; use for gradual improvements.
skill.summary: Improve code structure and readability through safe, incremental refactoring with preserved behavior.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 58 potential issues (42 external_commands, 1 filesystem, 15 blocker patterns). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the SKILL.md file contains only educational documentation with markdown code blocks showing refactoring before/after examples. The detected patterns (backticks, regex) exist in documentation text, not executable code. No actual security risks present.

content.value_statement: Legacy code becomes hard to maintain and extend. This skill applies proven refactoring techniques to improve structure and readability while preserving behavior.
actual_capabilities: Identify and explain common code smells like long methods, duplicated code, and large classes
Extract methods and classes to improve code organization
Introduce type safety with interfaces and type definitions
Apply design patterns like Strategy and Chain of Responsibility
Eliminate magic numbers and primitive obsession
Replace nested conditionals with guard clauses
limitations: Cannot execute or test the refactored code - requires user to run tests
Works with code provided in the conversation context only
Does not rewrite entire codebases - focuses on gradual improvements
Cannot access external repositories or file systems directly

use_cases.title: Improve Legacy Code
use_cases.description: Transform hard-to-maintain legacy code into clean, well-structured code by identifying smells and applying targeted refactoring techniques.

use_cases.title: Learn Refactoring Patterns
use_cases.description: Study common refactoring patterns with before-and-after examples to build skills in recognizing and fixing code quality issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Feature Addition
use_cases.description: Restructure code to make it easier to add new features by improving modularity and reducing coupling between components.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Code Smells
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code and identify any code smells. For each smell, explain what type it is and suggest a refactoring approach: [paste code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Get an overview of issues before making changes

prompt_templates.title: Extract Method Refactoring
prompt_templates.prompt: This function is too long and does multiple things. Help me extract it into smaller, focused functions with clear responsibilities: [paste function]

prompt_templates.title: Introduce Type Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this untyped JavaScript code to TypeScript with proper interfaces and type definitions. Explain each type choice: [paste code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Add type safety to existing code

prompt_templates.title: Apply Design Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: This code has complex conditional logic that would benefit from a design pattern. Analyze the code and suggest an appropriate pattern with implementation: [paste code]

output_examples.input: Review this 150-line function for code smells
output_examples.output: Identified 4 code smells: 1) Long Method - function exceeds 50 lines, 2) Duplicate Logic - same calculation appears 3 times, 3) Magic Numbers - unexplained values like 0.15 and 86400000, 4) Nested Conditionals - 5 levels deep. Recommended refactorings: Extract Method for the calculation logic, Introduce Constants for magic numbers, Replace Nested Conditional with Guard Clauses.

output_examples.input: Extract methods from this order processing function
output_examples.output: Created 5 focused functions: fetchOrder() handles data retrieval, validateOrder() checks business rules, calculatePricing() computes totals, updateInventory() manages stock, createShipment() handles fulfillment. Main processOrder() now orchestrates these steps clearly.

best_practices: Always run tests after each small refactoring step to ensure behavior is preserved
Commit to version control before and after each safe refactoring state
Focus on one code smell at a time rather than making multiple changes simultaneously
anti_patterns: Refactoring without tests - this is editing, not refactoring
Changing behavior while refactoring - keep functionality identical
Refactoring critical production code under tight deadlines without safety nets

faq.question: What is the difference between refactoring and rewriting?
faq.answer: Refactoring improves internal structure without changing external behavior. Rewriting means creating new code from scratch. Refactoring is gradual evolution; rewriting is revolution.

faq.question: When should I NOT refactor code?
faq.answer: Avoid refactoring when: code works and will not change again, you lack tests for critical code, you are under a tight deadline, or there is no clear purpose for the refactoring.

faq.question: How do I know if my code needs refactoring?
faq.answer: Look for code smells: functions over 50 lines, duplicated code, long parameter lists, magic numbers, nested conditionals, large classes, or difficulty adding new features.

faq.question: Is refactoring worth the time investment?
faq.answer: Yes, for code that will change again. Refactoring reduces future development time by making code easier to understand and modify. The investment pays off over the code lifecycle.

faq.question: Can this skill refactor any programming language?
faq.answer: The refactoring principles are language-agnostic and apply to most languages. The skill provides examples in JavaScript and TypeScript but concepts transfer to other languages.

faq.question: Should I refactor before or after adding a new feature?
faq.answer: Follow the rule: First refactor to make adding the feature easy, then add the feature. This is part of Test-Driven Development and keeps code clean as it evolves.
